title: Internal Memo: Template for Next Round of Practice Summaries
author: Wayne Kernochan
abstract: An internal Aberdeen Group memorandum from analyst Wayne Kernochan to Peter Kastner proposing a revised template for Aberdeen Practice Summary documents. The memo provides a detailed five-chapter master outline with worked examples drawn from NOS (Network Operating System) and NT/Intel server practice areas, and argues for recasting Practice Summaries as sales-oriented hot-topics vehicles. Two template versions are offered: a lightly revised version with prior-year examples and a more substantial revision emphasizing hot topics for 1999.
importance_rationale: Rare internal document revealing Aberdeen Group's research methodology and analyst workflow; documents how practice summaries were structured and the internal debate over research vs. sales orientation.
relevance_rationale: The master outline methodology (executive summary / findings / category definition / issues / supplier abstracts) remains a standard structure for IT analyst research reports; methodological interest is high.
prescience_rationale: Kernochan's prediction that NT Server would face scalability challenges before Windows 2000 arrived proved accurate; his view that NOSes would strengthen rather than fade due to internet growth was prescient.
